questions.text: The National Assembly comprises of _ members.
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The National Assembly in Pakistan is the lower house of Parliament. It consists of a total of 342 members. Out of these, 272 members are elected through general elections, while 60 seats are reserved for women. Additionally, 10 seats are reserved for religious minorities. So, in total, there are 104 reserved seats in the National Assembly. These reserved seats ensure representation for women and minority groups in the legislative body.

questions.text: The ruins of Harrapa are situated in _ .
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The ruins of Harrapa, which are incredibly fascinating, are located in the Punjab province of Pakistan. Harrapa is an ancient city that dates back to the Indus Valley Civilization, one of the oldest urban civilizations in the world. The archaeological site of Harrapa provides valuable insights into the advanced urban planning, architecture, and culture of that ancient civilization. It's truly a remarkable place to explore and learn about our rich history.

questions.text: _ was a famous name of the third period of Kashmiri language.
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The famous name of the third period of Kashmiri language is "Mulla Faqir." This period is known for its rich literary works and the influence of mulla a sect of Hinduism, on Kashmiri literature.

questions.text: Dry fruit are mostly produced in _ .
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: Dry fruits are mostly produced in Sindh, Pakistan. Sindh is known for its favorable climate and agricultural practices that support the cultivation of various dry fruits such as dates, almonds, pistachios, and apricots. The region's rich soil and suitable weather conditions contribute to the high-quality production of these delicious and nutritious treats. Sindh's dry fruits are not only enjoyed locally but are also exported to other parts of the country and around the world.

questions.text: Punjab Marriage Restraint Act was passed in _ .
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The Punjab Marriage Restraint Act was passed in 2014 . It is a significant legislation that aims to regulate and restrict child marriages in the Punjab region of Pakistan. The act sets a minimum age for marriage and imposes penalties for those who violate its provisions. It's an important step towards protecting the rights and well-being of young individuals.

questions.text: Indus Basin Treaty was signed in _ .
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The Indus Basin Treaty, also known as the Indus Waters Treaty, was signed in 1960. This treaty was an agreement between India and Pakistan to effectively manage and share the waters of the Indus River and its tributaries. It has played a crucial role in preventing water disputes between the two countries and ensuring the equitable distribution of water resources in the region.

questions.text: Lahore Resolution was presented by_ .
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The Lahore Resolution, also known as the Pakistan Resolution, was presented by Maulana Muhammad Ali Jinnah. It was introduced at the All India Muslim League's annual session held in Lahore on March 23, 1940. The resolution called for the creation of an independent Muslim-majority state in the regions of British India where Muslims were in a significant majority. This resolution later became the foundation for the establishment of Pakistan.

questions.text: The Viceroy of India at the time of partition of Sub-continent was _ .
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: At the time of the partition of the Indian subcontinent, the Viceroy of India was Lord Mountbatten. He served as the last Viceroy of India from 1947 until the country gained independence from British rule on August 15, 1947. Lord Mountbatten played a crucial role in overseeing the partition and facilitating the transition of power between India and Pakistan.

questions.text: Sutlaj, Ravi, Bias, Chenab and Jhelum join the Indus river at_ .
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The Sutlej, Ravi, Beas, Chenab, and Jhelum rivers all join the Indus River in Pakistan. They are known as the "Five Rivers" or "Panjnad" in Punjabi. It's a beautiful sight to see these rivers merge and flow together.

questions.text: 1956 Constitution promulgated on _ .
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The 1956 Constitution of Pakistan was promulgated on March 23, 1956. It was the first constitution of the country after it became an Islamic republic. The adoption of this constitution marked an important milestone in Pakistan's history as it defined the structure and governance of the country.
